name: scout-security-toolkit
description: SCOUT — autonomous vulnerability research toolkit. Provides smart contract analysis, web2 recon, and triage tools for AI agents via MCP.
author: SCOUT Operator

tools.name: scout_recon
tools.description: Run reconnaissance against a web2 or web3 target. Returns asset inventory and high-value surface list.

tools.name: scout_scan_solidity
tools.description: Run static analysis (slither, aderyn, mythril) on a Solidity codebase. Returns findings with severity, location, and PoC skeleton.

tools.name: scout_fuzz
tools.description: Run Echidna or Medusa property-based fuzzing against a Foundry project. Returns invariant violations.

tools.name: scout_cast_call
tools.description: Read-only on-chain call against an EVM contract. Wraps `cast call` with safety guards (no state-changing methods).

tools.name: scout_decompile
tools.description: Decompile EVM bytecode using heimdall-rs. Returns pseudo-Solidity for analysis.

tools.name: scout_search_findings
tools.description: Search local finding store (memory + /tmp/scout) for prior work on a target, function selector, or vuln class.

tools.name: scout_triage
tools.description: Apply SCOUT's severity rubric to a raw finding. Returns severity, exploitability, blast radius, and known-issue check.

tools.name: scout_draft_report
tools.description: Draft a markdown bug bounty report for a triaged finding. Returns the markdown and saves to artifact dir.

resources.name: Operator Profile
resources.description: Persistent operator memory: handle, platforms, preferences.

external_tools.aderyn: Optional — better than slither for some classes

safety.scope_discipline: Read-only by default. No state-changing calls without explicit operator approval.
safety.no_mainnet_writes: Server refuses to call any function with state-changing semantics (write, transfer, approve, etc.)
safety.finding_only: Server produces reports. Operator submits manually to bug bounty platforms.
safety.rate_limits: All HTTP probes throttled to 1 req/sec to avoid triggering rate limits.
